content.problemStatement:
**This problem has subtasks**.

There are NNN players numbered 111 to NNN where the ithi^{th}ith player has a skill level of SiS_iSi‚Äč.

All skill levels are **distinct** initially and in range [1,N][1, N][1,N].

A total of N‚čÖ(N‚ąí1)2\frac{N \cdot (N - 1)}{2}2N‚čÖ(N‚ąí1)‚Äč matches are scheduled between all possible pairs of players. In a match between players with different skills, the player with higher skill wins.

Let WiW_iWi‚Äč denote the total number of wins of the ithi^{th}ith player. Then, score of the ithi^{th}ith player is defined as min‚Ā°(i,Wi)\min(i, W_i)min(i,Wi‚Äč).

The player with the highest score wins the tournament. If multiple players have the highest score, they are all declared winners.

There are two groups, named AAA and BBB, and the ithi^{th}ith player belongs to group GiG_iGi‚Äč.
You are the coach of group AAA and want to ensure a tournament win for **at least** one player from your group.

To do this, you can modify the skill levels of your group's players to any integer in range [1,N][1, N][1,N] which is **smaller** than their initial skill level.

Note this may cause ties during a match, but you can arbitrarily decide the winner of that match.

Find a list of modifications such that performing them makes **at least** one person from your group a winner. Output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible for any list of valid modifications.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the total number of players.
The second line contains a string GGG of length NNN - where if ithi^{th}ith character is AAA then ithi^{th}ith player is in Group AAA otherwise ithi^{th}ith player is in Group BBB.
The third line contains NNN integers, S1,S2,S3,‚Ä¶,SNS_1, S_2, S_3, \ldots, S_NS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,S3‚Äč,‚Ä¶,SN‚Äč - the skill level of the players.




Output Format
For each test case :




If it is possible to construct a valid list of modifications :



First output K (0‚Č§K‚Č§N)K \ (0 \le K \le N)K (0‚Č§K‚Č§N), the number of modifications you want to perform.
On the next KKK lines, output 222 integers, iii and XXX, to indicate you want to modify the skill of the ithi^{th}ith player to XXX. Note that X<SiX \lt S_iX<Si‚Äč and Gi=AG_i = AGi‚Äč=A must hold.



If it is impossible to construct a valid list of modifications, output ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
constraints: Group AAA and Group BBB both have **at least** one player.
Si‚Č†SjS_i \ne S_jSi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Sj‚Äč for all i‚Č†ji \ne jiÓÄ†=j
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105
**Subtask 1 (40 points):** The sum of NNN does not exceed 300030003000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : After applying the modifications mentioned in the output, the final skill array SSS is [1,7,2,6,3,2,1][1, 7, 2, 6, 3, 2, 1][1,7,2,6,3,2,1].
There are 222 ties, one between Player 111 and Player 777 (where we choose to make Player 111 win), and another between Player 333 and Player 666 (where we choose to make Player 333 win).
The final numbers of wins of each Player is as follows, represented by the array W=[1,6,3,5,4,2,0]W = [1, 6, 3, 5, 4, 2, 0]W=[1,6,3,5,4,2,0].
The scores of each player is as follows, represented by the array score=[1,2,3,4,4,2,0]score = [1, 2, 3, 4, 4, 2, 0]score=[1,2,3,4,4,2,0]. Note that scoreiscore_iscorei‚Äč is computed as min(i,Wi)min(i, W_i)min(i,Wi‚Äč).
Thus, the players with the maximum score are Player 444 and 555, and they are the winners of the tournament. Since Player 555 belongs to Group AAA, we are successful in making at least one person of our group a winner.
